disciplineChoices.name: Keen Sense
disciplineChoices.description: Sharpened perception that reveals hidden dangers and details others would miss.

disciplineChoices.name: Shadow Step
disciplineChoices.description: The ability to move without sound and pass unseen, even in the open.

disciplineChoices.name: Mending
disciplineChoices.description: The art of healing wounds, your own or another's, without herbs or potions.

disciplineChoices.name: Tracking
disciplineChoices.description: Skill in hunting, following trails, and living off the wilderness.

scenes.narrativeText: The dirt road forks beneath a lightning-split oak. To the west, a narrow trail vanishes into a shadowed thicket. To the east, smoke rises faintly over a ridge, carrying the tang of old iron. Your pack is light, but your resolve is lighter still after three days without proper rest.

choices.choiceText: Take the western trail into the thicket
Head east toward the ridge and the smoke

scenes.narrativeText: Branches close overhead, and the light dims to a green murk. Something rustles just beyond sight, matching your pace step for step. The path ahead splits again around a fallen trunk thick with moss.

choices.choiceText: Push on along the muddy path

scenes.narrativeText: Cresting the ridge, you find the source of the smoke: a squat, tusked brute crouched over a smouldering campfire, gnawing on a blackened bone. It rises with a snarl the moment it catches your scent, hefting a notched cleaver.

combatChoices.notes: Fights with a notched cleaver; no special resistances.
Fights with a notched cleaver; no special resistances.
Evasion available only to those trained in Shadow Step.
combatChoices.choiceText: Slip away into the brush before the brute closes in

scenes.narrativeText: Past the ridge, a cairn of grey stones marks a hollow in the hillside. Wedged between two slabs, a rust-eaten key catches the last light. As you pry it free, the wind seems to hold its breath, as if the vale itself takes note of your passing.

choices.choiceText: Leave the hollow and continue on

scenes.narrativeText: The gates of Eldermoor rise before you at last, torches guttering against the coming dusk. The guard at the postern recognizes the old key in your hand and waves you through without a word. Whatever waits inside, the road behind you is finally done.
